DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND DECLARATION AND CHARLES AY'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, REPORTER: DAVID A. DISBROW, CSR #7768 (TEL: 415-298-1538)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUE 2 (ASBESTOSIS). AS TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT, DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN PURSUANT TO THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR V. ATLANTIC RICHFIELD CO. (2001) 25 CAL.4TH 826. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IN THIS ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION, DEFENDANT'S RESPONSES TO STANDARD INTERROGATORIES (EXHIBIT Q), AND ERNEST DEMARSE'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY (EXHIBIT P) CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER PLAINTIFF WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS (GOODYEARITE SHEET GASKET MATERIAL) ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. AS TO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS RELATED TO ASBESTOSIS, THE MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF IS BARRED FROM SEEKING DAMAGES RELATED TO ASBESTOSIS. THIS RULING SHALL NOT BE CONSIDERED A DETERMINATION WHETHER PLAINTIFF MAY INTRODUCE EVIDENCE REGARDING AN ASBESTOSIS DIAGNOSIS AT TRIAL. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED AS TO DEFENDANT'S EXHIBIT L AND OVERRULED AS TO EXHIBIT M. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S EXHIBITS A, H, I, J, K, L, M, P, Q AND SUSTAINED AS TO EXHIBITS N, O, R. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. HEARING ON THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO ISSUE 1 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) IS ORDERED CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 3, 2017 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. DEFENDANT'S SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING IS DUE BY CLOSE OF BUSINESS ON 9/29/17 WHILE PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSE DUE BY NOON ON 10/2/17, LIMITED TO 5 PAGES EACH. JUDGE: CYNTHIA MING-MEI LEE, REPORTER: DAVID A. DISBROW, CSR #7768 (TEL: 415-298-1538)

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PUNITIVE DAMAGE

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's objection to the Court considering Plaintiff's Exhibit D (Plaintiff's Supplemental Responses to Defendant's Interrogatories, dated September 1, 2017) is sustained. Plaintiff's Exhibit D was not cited to in Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts in Opposition to Defendant's motion for Summary Adjudication of punitive damages as required by CCP 437c(b)(3). Plaintiff may not rely on Exhibit D in opposition to the motion. As to summary adjudication of Plaintiff's claim for punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff's Exhibits S (Deposition of Holthouser) and V (Deposition of Demarse) create a triable issue on punitive damages. Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary adjudication on punitive damages is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. APPEARANCES:
Megan F. Clark of GORDON & REES LLP (510-463-8600), appearing for defendant / moving party The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company
Garen Nazarian of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Leslie Strothers

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE GOODYEAR TIRE & RUBBER COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant The Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to Issue 2 (asbestosis). As to summary judgment, defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Plaintiff's deposition testimony in this action, plaintiff's declaration, defendant's responses to standard interrogatories (Exhibit Q), and Ernest DeMarse's deposition testimony (Exhibit P) create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Goodyearite sheet gasket material) attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of plaintiff's claims related to asbestosis, the motion is granted to the extent that plaintiff is barred from seeking damages related to asbestosis. This ruling shall not be considered a determination whether plaintiff may introduce evidence regarding an asbestosis diagnosis at trial. Plaintiff's objections are sustained as to defendant's Exhibit L and overruled as to Exhibit M. Defendant's objections are overruled as to plaintiff's Exhibits A, H, I, J, K, L, M, P, Q and sustained as to Exhibits N, O, R. Order signed in open court.
Hearing on the alternate motion for summary adjudication as to Issue 1 (punitive damages) is ordered continued to October 3, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Defendant's supplemental briefing is due by close of business on 9/29/17 while plaintiff's response due by noon on 10/2/17, limited to 5 pages each. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. APPEARANCES:
Abigail P. Adams and Lindsay Weiss of MANION GAYNOR & MANNING LLP (415-527-2803), appearing for defendant / moving party Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.
Tiffany Viveros of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Leslie Strothers

RULING:
DEFENDANT TEMPORARY PLANT CLEANERS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. While defendant may have demonstrated that Plant Maintenance employees did not expose plaintiff to asbestos-containing products or materials at the Tosco refinery in 1984, defendant did not demonstrate that such site was the only time plaintiff encountered Plant Maintenance employees. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and defendant's failure sufficiently question him on the topic preclude defendant from sustaining its initial burden. However, if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the declaration of Charles Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation attributable to defendant. The Court must liberally construe plaintiffs' evidence and make all reasonable inferences in plaintiffs' favor. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit V through Z are sustained. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and Charles Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: David A. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF.
Appearances: Kimberly Chu of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and moving party; Laura Przetak of Spanos Przetak (510.250.0200) designated defense counsel; See sign-in sheet for counsel not listed in the mini-minutes.
Argued and the Court modifies its ruling as follows: Granted pursuant to CCP 36(a) and (d). The trial date is Oct-2-2017 at 11:15am in Dept. 503. Last day pursuant to CCP 36(f) is Oct-12-2017. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all SF Local Rules and CRC. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is Sep-28-2017. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the CCP, unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of 4 summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the CCP, whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than Aug-18-2017. Responses to the demand are due no later than Sep-1-2017. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by Sep-25-2017 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on Sep-28-2017. Fact discovery cut-off date is Sep-15-2017. Expert discovery cut-off date is Sep-29-2017. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than Jul-14-2017. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than 5 days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than Jun-22-2017 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than Jun-28-2017 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further CMC on Jul-5-2017 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any CMC statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a MSC with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and SF Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on Oct-2-2017 at 1:30p.m. in Dept. 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by Sep-28-2017 at 2:00p.m. The following party's response to plaintiff's motion were not considered for failure to provide the Court with courtesy copies: Anheuser-Busch. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed. Ms. Chu confirms that when pathology is received, designated defense counsel will be notified. Defendants have requested plaintiff's trial preservation testimony be produced. They will set up an informal discovery conference before filing a motion on the issue. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Reporter: Kim Richardson, CSR 5915 (Aiken Welch)
